articles.title: Anthropic essentially bans OpenClaw from Claude by making subscribers pay extra
articles.summary: Anthropic will require separate pay-as-you-go billing for OpenClaw users starting April 4th, effectively making the third-party tool more expensive and limiting subscription access.

articles.title: OpenAI’s AGI boss is taking a leave of absence
articles.summary: OpenAI's CEO of AGI deployment Fidji Simo is taking medical leave for several weeks due to a neuroimmune condition. CMO Kate Rouch is also stepping down for health reasons.

articles.title: Apple’s best product ever
articles.summary: Apple celebrated its 50th anniversary by asking users to rank the company's 50 best products, receiving over 1.6 million votes to determine the most iconic creations.

articles.title: Anthropic is having a moment in the private markets; SpaceX could spoil the party
articles.summary: Anthropic leads the private share market with active trading, while OpenAI loses momentum. SpaceX's anticipated IPO could significantly reshape the competitive landscape.

articles.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
articles.summary: OpenAI's COO Brad Lightcap assumes new special projects role. CMO Kate Rouch departs temporarily for cancer recovery, planning to return.

articles.title: Anthropic buys biotech startup Coefficient Bio in $400M deal: Reports
articles.summary: Anthropic acquires stealth biotech startup Coefficient Bio for $400 million in stock deal, expanding AI capabilities in biological research and drug discovery.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a San Francisco cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million Series B to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS. The funding capitalizes on developer frustration with legacy cloud platforms' complexity and cost.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing sparks developer backlash. Open-source alternative Goose offers identical functionality for free, running locally without subscription fees or rate limits.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign using AI tokens and coding challenges. The creative stunt decoded to a puzzle about Berlin nightclub selection, attracting thousands of engineer applicants.

articles.title: OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
articles.summary: OpenClaw, a viral AI tool with 347,000 GitHub stars, poses security risks by requiring broad system access to control computers and interact with apps. A recently patched vulnerability highlights dangers of granting such extensive permissions.

articles.title: New Rowhammer attacks give complete control of machines running Nvidia GPUs
articles.summary: New Rowhammer attacks exploit memory bit flips in Nvidia GPUs to grant malicious users complete root control of shared cloud machines. The vulnerability affects expensive high-performance GPUs commonly shared in cloud environments.

articles.title: Quantum computers need vastly fewer resources than thought to break vital encryption
articles.summary: New research shows quantum computers need far fewer resources than previously thought to break elliptic-curve encryption, with approaches achieving 100x resource reductions and cracking 256-bit ECC in days.

articles.title: Four things we’d need to put data centers in space
articles.summary: SpaceX filed to launch orbital data centers to support AI growth while avoiding environmental damage on Earth. Major tech companies like Amazon and Google are exploring space-based computing infrastructure.

articles.title: The Download: plastic’s problem with fuel prices, and SpaceX’s blockbuster IPO
articles.summary: Rising fuel prices from geopolitical conflict threaten plastic supply chains, as petrochemical costs increase. Americans may soon face higher plastic product prices due to supply chain disruptions.

articles.title: Fuel prices are soaring. Plastic could be next.
articles.summary: Rising fuel prices from Middle East tensions threaten plastic supply chains, as petrochemicals become scarce. Plastic production's heavy reliance on oil could trigger broader economic impacts beyond energy costs.

articles.title: Welcome Gemma 4: Frontier multimodal intelligence on device
articles.summary: Google introduces Gemma 4, a frontier multimodal AI model designed to run efficiently on-device. The advancement enables powerful intelligence capabilities directly on personal devices without requiring cloud connectivity.

articles.title: Holo3: Breaking the Computer Use Frontier
articles.summary: Holo3 represents a breakthrough in computer use technology, advancing AI capabilities to interact with digital systems more effectively and naturally.

articles.title: Falcon Perception
articles.summary: Falcon Perception advances AI visual recognition technology, enabling faster and more accurate object detection in images and video streams.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Microsoft's Kevin Scott discusses the future of AI technology and upcoming developments in the field, exploring what lies ahead for artificial intelligence.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creative capabilities, streamlining content creation and business processes.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft releases its 'farm of the future' toolkit as open source, enabling broader access to agricultural AI technology and sustainable farming solutions.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduced two new inference tiers for the Gemini API—Flex and Priority—to balance cost and latency tradeoffs for developers.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids introduces free AI-powered video creation and editing features using Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1, enabling high-quality video generation at no cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map designed to protect the country's forests.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most capable open-source models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, offering improved intelligence byte-for-byte.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash Live voice model improves precision and reduces latency for more natural, fluid voice interactions.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches AI manipulation risks in finance and health sectors, developing new safety measures to protect people from harmful AI-driven manipulation.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family, while discussions covered attention residuals and Mamba 3 developments.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launched DLSS 5, an AI-powered graphics technology that uses generative AI to enhance photorealism in real-time gaming up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting conditions.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: LWiAI Podcast #237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub code review pricing, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: KiloClaw targets shadow AI with autonomous agent governance
articles.summary: KiloClaw is an enterprise platform launched by Kilo to govern autonomous agents and manage shadow AI deployments. It addresses the 'Bring Your Own AI' trend where employees bypass official procurement to deploy agents on personal infrastructure, exposing proprietary data.

articles.title: 5 best practices to secure AI systems
articles.summary: AI systems require multi-layered security strategies including data protection, access control, and monitoring. Five foundational practices address emerging risks from AI's expanding capabilities and attack surface.

articles.title: China’s Five-Year Plan details the targets for AI deployment
articles.summary: China's 15th Five-Year Plan prioritizes AI alongside quantum computing and biotechnology through 2030, focusing on high-performance chips, software, model architectures, and supporting infrastructure like 5G+ and 6G networks.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur. This work addresses debugging challenges in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses localization, destination identification, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights via reinforcement learning.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 model advances video self-supervised learning by balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, solving a long-standing challenge in AI.

articles.title: Multi-level AI prompt engineering: A new tool for scientific discovery
articles.summary: Multi-level prompt engineering and parabolic extrapolation enabled an LLM to function as a theoretical collaborator, producing a testable multiverse model for scientific discovery.

articles.title: Netflix AI Team Just Open-Sourced VOID: an AI Model That Erases Objects From Videos — Physics and All
articles.summary: Netflix's VOID model automatically removes objects from videos while accounting for physical interactions like gravity and shadows, solving a problem that previously required weeks of manual VFX work.

articles.title: How to Build Production-Ready Agentic Systems with Z.AI GLM-5 Using Thinking Mode, Tool Calling, Streaming, and Multi-Turn Workflows
articles.summary: Tutorial on building production-ready agentic systems using Z.AI's GLM-5 model, covering features like thinking mode, tool calling, streaming, and multi-turn conversations for real-world applications.

articles.title: Google DeepMind’s Research Lets an LLM Rewrite Its Own Game Theory Algorithms — And It Outperformed the Experts
articles.summary: Google DeepMind's AlphaEvolve uses an LLM-powered evolutionary agent to automatically design game theory algorithms for multi-agent reinforcement learning, outperforming manually-created baselines in imperfect-information games like poker.

articles.title: FTC reports a surge in $220M job fraud - here's how to vet listings, according to recruiters
articles.summary: The FTC reports a $220M surge in job fraud. Recruiters advise verifying job listings carefully in today's competitive market.

articles.title: LG G6 vs. LG G5: I compared the flagship OLED TVs, and there's a surprise winner
articles.summary: LG G6 and G5 OLED TVs are compared to highlight key differences for potential upgraders, with an unexpected winner emerging from the flagship models.

articles.title: Is increasing VRAM finally worth it? I ran the numbers on my Windows 11 PC
articles.summary: Virtual RAM can boost PC performance when resources are limited, but it cannot replace physical RAM. The article examines whether increasing VRAM is worthwhile on Windows 11.

articles.title: 7 Machine Learning Trends to Watch in 2026
articles.summary: Machine learning systems are evolving from background dashboards to more visible roles. Key trends for 2026 include advanced model deployment and increased AI integration across industries.

articles.title: Building a ‘Human-in-the-Loop’ Approval Gate for Autonomous Agents
articles.summary: Agentic AI systems implement human-in-the-loop approval gates through state-managed interruptions, pausing agent execution pipelines for human review and oversight.

articles.title: From Prompt to Prediction: Understanding Prefill, Decode, and the KV Cache in LLMs
articles.summary: Article explains LLM inference phases: prefill attention mechanism, decode phase, and KV cache optimization for efficient token generation during prediction.

articles.title: DenseNet Paper Walkthrough: All Connected
articles.summary: DenseNet addresses the vanishing gradient problem in deep neural networks by improving weight updates during training. The architecture enables better model improvement through enhanced connectivity.

articles.title: I Replaced Vector DBs with Google’s Memory Agent Pattern for my notes in Obsidian
articles.summary: A developer replaced vector databases with Google's Memory Agent Pattern for Obsidian notes, eliminating the need for embeddings and complex similarity search infrastructure.

articles.title: Linear Regression Is Actually a Projection Problem (Part 2: From Projections to Predictions)
articles.summary: This article explains linear regression through vector projections, showing how least squares can be understood as a projection problem that enables predictions.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: NVIDIA and Google optimized Gemma 4, a small, fast open model for local AI agents, enabling efficient performance across devices from RTX PCs to data centers without relying on cloud computing.

articles.title: Press Start on April: GeForce NOW Brings 10 Games to the Cloud
articles.summary: GeForce NOW launches 10 new games in April, including Capcom's PRAGMATA and Arknights: Endfield, a new 3D real-time strategy title.

articles.title: Efficiency at Scale: NVIDIA, Energy Leaders Accelerating Power‑Flexible AI Factories to Fortify the Grid
articles.summary: NVIDIA and Emerald AI unveiled flexible AI factories that operate as intelligent grid assets, enabling efficient power management and grid reliability. This approach helps large AI deployments connect faster and operate more sustainably.

articles.title: Simulate realistic users to evaluate multi-turn AI agents in Strands Evals
articles.summary: Strands Evals framework enables systematic evaluation of multi-turn AI agents by simulating realistic user interactions, assessing helpfulness, faithfulness, and tool usage across complex conversations.

articles.title: Scaling seismic foundation models on AWS: Distributed training with Amazon SageMaker HyperPod and expanding context windows
articles.summary: TGS partnered with AWS to optimize seismic foundation models using Amazon SageMaker HyperPod, achieving near-linear scaling for distributed training and reducing training time from 6 months to 5 days.

articles.title: Control which domains your AI agents can access
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore allows controlled AI agent web access through AWS Network Firewall domain filtering in VPCs, enhancing security and compliance while enabling research automation and data gathering capabilities.

articles.title: Accelerating Vision AI Pipelines with Batch Mode VC-6 and NVIDIA Nsight
articles.summary: NVIDIA's batch mode VC-6 and Nsight tools accelerate vision AI pipelines by optimizing decode, preprocessing, and GPU scheduling to match improved model throughput.

articles.title: Bringing AI Closer to the Edge and On-Device with Gemma 4
articles.summary: Google launches Gemma 4, multimodal multilingual AI models scaling from data centers to edge devices. Designed to run on NVIDIA Blackwell and Jetson platforms across deployments.

articles.title: Achieving Single-Digit Microsecond Latency Inference for Capital Markets
articles.summary: NVIDIA enables single-digit microsecond latency for AI inference in capital markets, allowing algorithmic traders to respond faster to market events using advanced neural network models instead of traditional specialized hardware.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon mitigation technology and sharing it openly to reduce its environmental footprint.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with unconventional background developed innovative extended reality technology by applying unique perspectives and skills to XR challenges, demonstrating how diverse experience drives technological breakthroughs.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers develop methods to identify feature interactions at scale in Large Language Models, advancing interpretability to understand how these complex systems make decisions and improve AI safety.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems uses encoders and noise models to optimize how well measurements distinguish objects. The approach quantifies measurement quality through information estimation.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new reinforcement learning algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, offering better scalability for long-horizon tasks without TD learning's limitations.
